name: Lemon Wonder Cake/1953 Pillsbury Winner
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk (or sour milk: mix 1 cup sweet milk with 1 tablespoon vinegar and let sit for 5 minutes)
recipeInstructions:
A winner in Senior division of the 1953 Pillsbury bakeoff.
Grind pulp and rind of lemon with 1 cup dates and 1/4 cup of dry ingredients (flour, baking powder, baking soda).
Cream together the butter and both sugars; add eggs and beat for one minute. Add buttermilk alternately with the flour mixture to the egg mixture. Pour batter into two well greased and lightly floured 8-inch round pans. Bake in preheated oven at 350°F for 30-35 minutes.
Caramel Frosting: Combine 1 cup sugar, 1 cup firmly packed brown sugar, and 2/3 cup sour cream in a saucepan. Cook to soft ball stage (234°F). Remove from heat and cool to lukewarm (110°F), then add 1 teaspoon vanilla. Beat until thick enough to spread. Thin with a small amount of cream if necessary.
